presets.name: Turfgrass
presets.description: Lawn needs consistent moisture to stay green. Sierra runs more often and keeps the rootzone comfortable.

presets.name: Fescue lawn
presets.description: Fine and tall fescues handle some drought but prefer even moisture. Deep, infrequent watering encourages deep roots.

presets.name: Clover & mixed groundcover
presets.description: Clover and low groundcovers are drought-tolerant once established. Sierra waters sparingly to keep them lush without overwatering.

presets.name: Leafy vegetables
presets.description: Lettuce, spinach, and chard are thirsty and shallow-rooted. They need steady moisture and will wilt quickly if left dry.

presets.name: Fruiting vegetables
presets.description: Tomatoes, peppers, and zucchini need deep, consistent watering — especially when fruiting. Irregular moisture leads to blossom-end rot.

presets.name: Root vegetables
presets.description: Carrots, beets, and radishes need even soil moisture to swell properly. Inconsistent watering causes cracking and forking.

presets.name: Brassicas
presets.description: Cabbage, broccoli, and cauliflower are heavy feeders that like consistent moisture. Dry spells cause bolting and poor head formation.

presets.name: Cucumbers & squash
presets.description: Cucumbers and summer squash are fast-growing and need steady moisture. They are sensitive to dry spells during fruit development.

presets.name: Herbs — moderate (basil, parsley, mint)
presets.description: Basil, parsley, and mint like steady moisture but not wet feet. Sierra keeps the balance without babysitting.

presets.name: Herbs — dry-loving (rosemary, thyme, oregano)
presets.description: Mediterranean herbs hate wet roots. They evolved in thin, dry soils and produce more flavour when kept on the dry side.

presets.name: Potted herbs
presets.description: Containers dry out much faster than garden beds. Sierra checks in more frequently to keep potted herbs from wilting between waterings.

presets.name: Potted citrus
presets.description: Lemon and orange trees in containers dry out faster than in-ground plants. Sierra adjusts for the smaller soil volume.

presets.name: Strawberries
presets.description: Strawberries need consistent moisture, especially during fruit set. Dry spells reduce yield; soggy soil causes crown rot.

presets.name: Blueberries
presets.description: Blueberries prefer consistently moist, acidic soil. They have shallow roots that stress quickly in dry conditions.

presets.name: Grapevines
presets.description: Established vines are drought-tolerant but benefit from deep, infrequent watering during fruit development. Mild stress at the right time concentrates sugars.

presets.name: Flowering perennials
presets.description: Roses, hydrangeas, and daylilies bloom better with consistent moisture. Sierra watches the soil so you can watch the flowers.

presets.name: Annual bedding plants
presets.description: Petunias, marigolds, and impatiens are shallow-rooted and thirsty. They need regular attention through the season.

presets.name: Bulbs (tulips, daffodils, dahlias)
presets.description: Bulbs need moisture during active growth but must dry out after flowering to avoid rot. Sierra backs off automatically in late season.

presets.name: Roses
presets.description: Roses reward deep watering at the base — they hate wet foliage. Sierra keeps the rootzone moist while avoiding the splash that spreads black spot.

presets.name: Mediterranean shrubs
presets.description: Lavender, rosemary, and sage prefer it on the drier side. They reward patience and dislike soggy roots.

presets.name: Young trees (first 2 years)
presets.description: Newly planted trees need deep, regular watering to establish their root system. Sierra is more attentive until the tree can fend for itself.

presets.name: Established trees
presets.description: Mature trees have deep roots and can handle dry spells. Sierra waters deeply but infrequently to encourage continued root depth.

presets.name: Formal hedges (box, privet, yew)
presets.description: Dense hedges have a high leaf area and need regular moisture to maintain tight, dense growth. They respond poorly to drought stress.

presets.name: Succulents & cacti
presets.description: Slow drinkers by nature. Sierra checks in every few days and lets the soil dry out completely between waterings.

presets.name: Aloe & agave
presets.description: Large-bodied succulents store water for weeks. Sierra gives them an occasional deep drink and then leaves them alone.

presets.name: Tropical potted plants
presets.description: Monstera, bird of paradise, and philodendrons like moist but well-draining soil. They do not tolerate sitting in water.

presets.name: Ferns & shade lovers
presets.description: Ferns need consistently moist soil and humidity. They are the most demanding of regular watering and will brown at the tips when underwatered.
